title: Bakery Logo
context: Sweet Crumb Bakery needs a new logo. The client sent over this brief. Something feels off — can you spot the problems?

requirements.text: Design a logo that feels modern and contemporary
requirements.explanation: Brand direction is a core requirement the designer must follow.

requirements.text: But also traditional and timeless — like grandma's kitchen
requirements.explanation: "Modern" and "traditional" are contradictory directions. This pair needs to be resolved before the design can proceed.

requirements.text: Use warm, bright colours like yellow and orange
requirements.explanation: Colour direction is a key brand decision that guides the design.

requirements.text: The logo must work in black and white for newspaper ads
requirements.explanation: Specifying bright colours while requiring black-and-white reproduction is contradictory — bright colours become meaningless in monochrome.

requirements.text: Include the bakery's founding year (1987) in the logo
requirements.explanation: Heritage detail is a nice addition but not essential to brand identity.

requirements.text: The logo should work at small sizes (favicon, social media profile)
requirements.explanation: Logo versatility across sizes is a technical requirement that must be designed for from the start.

teachingMoment: Contradictory visual requirements ("bright colours" vs "black and white only") are the most common first-brief failure. Always spot and flag these before starting work.
